section.name: postgresql-backup
section.summary: PostgreSQL backup and restore using pg_dump, with Docker exec pattern and dump integrity verification.

rule.statement: Always use the custom format (-Fc) for pg_dump; it is compressed, supports parallel restore, and enables selective table restore.
rule.rationale: Plain SQL dumps are large, uncompressed, and cannot restore individual tables. Custom format is smaller, faster to restore, and more flexible for targeted recovery.

rule.statement: Verify every dump immediately after creation with pg_restore --list; a corrupt dump discovered during a restore doubles the disaster.
rule.rationale: pg_dump can succeed (exit 0) but produce a truncated file if disk space runs out mid-write. --list reads the dump catalog without restoring data and fails on corruption.

example.code:
# Custom format: compressed, selective restore, preferred choice docker exec -t nero-postgres pg_dump -U nero -Fc nero_db > nero_db_$(date +%Y%m%d).dump
# Verify dump integrity immediately pg_restore --list nero_db_$(date +%Y%m%d).dump > /dev/null && echo "OK" || echo "CORRUPT"
# All databases (for full server backup) docker exec -t nero-postgres pg_dumpall -U nero > all_databases_$(date +%Y%m%d).sql
# Specific tables only docker exec -t nero-postgres pg_dump -U nero -Fc -t conversations -t messages nero_db > tables.dump

# Wrong: no verification; corrupt dumps discovered only at restore time docker exec -t nero-postgres pg_dump -U nero nero_db > backup.sql
# Wrong: plain SQL is large and cannot restore individual tables pg_dump -h localhost -U nero nero_db > backup.sql
example.note: Always verify. Always use -Fc unless you specifically need human-readable SQL for auditing purposes.

example.code:
# Restore custom format (clean = drop+recreate existing objects) docker exec -i nero-postgres pg_restore -U nero -d nero_db \ --clean --if-exists < nero_db.dump
# Restore specific table only docker exec -i nero-postgres pg_restore -U nero -d nero_db \ -t conversations nero_db.dump
# Restore plain SQL docker exec -i nero-postgres psql -U nero -d nero_db < backup.sql

antipattern.description: Restoring into a running production database without --clean and without --if-exists.
antipattern.reason: Without --clean, existing tables accumulate duplicate data. Without --if-exists, restore fails on the first table that does not yet exist, leaving the database in a partial state.

list.item: -Fd (directory): splits into files per table, suitable for parallel restore of very large databases
-Ft (tar): no compression, archive-compatible, supports selective restore
default (plain SQL): uncompressed, human-readable, no selective restore, largest output
